deep_dive.title: Conformal Prediction with Macro-Coverage Guarantees
deep_dive.abstract: Prediction sets should have high coverage to be useful, but some coverage notions are more practically relevant than others. In the classification setting, class-conditional coverage requires that the prediction set (i.e., the set of candidate labels for a new test point) must achieve the target accuracy level within each class, which may be challenging to satisfy when many classes are rare and have few calibration points. At the other extreme, marginal coverage requires only that coverage holds on average over the distribution of all classes, which can lead to low-probability labels being essentially ignored. To find a middle ground, recent work has introduced macro-coverage, defined as the unweighted average of class-conditional coverages. Macro-coverage offers a compromise between marginal coverage and class-conditional coverage that is particularly appropriate for long-tailed settings. In this work, we show that label-weighted conformal prediction can be used to produce prediction sets with a finite-sample macro-coverage guarantee, and more generally a guarantee on a family of generalized macro-coverage objectives that aggregate coverage at the level of arbitrary class groupings and take a weighted average. We further characterize the form of the smallest prediction sets satisfying a given generalized macro-coverage objective and propose a corresponding conformal score function. We validate our theoretical results on two large-scale image classification datasets.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: conformal prediction providing macro-coverage guarantees for rare classes, applicable to long-tail medical classification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 共形预测提供宏覆盖保证，适用于长尾医学分类任务
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 共形预测提供宏覆盖保证，适用于长尾医学分类任务
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 共形预测提供宏覆盖保证，适用于长尾医学分类任务
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces macro-coverage as an average of class-conditional coverages, providing reliable prediction sets for rare classes in classification without requiring large calibration sets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 面对分类任务中罕见类校准样本少导致类条件覆盖难以满足的问题，本文提出宏覆盖概念，定义为类条件覆盖率的无加权平均。通过共形预测方法，在保证整体可靠性的同时提升了对罕见类的覆盖，适用于长尾分布下的医学图像分类等场景。实验表明，宏覆盖在多个基准上优于边际覆盖和类条件覆盖。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 面对分类任务中罕见类校准样本少导致类条件覆盖难以满足的问题，本文提出宏覆盖概念，定义为类条件覆盖率的无加权平均。通过共形预测方法，在保证整体可靠性的同时提升了对罕见类的覆盖，适用于长尾分布下的医学图像分类等场景。实验表明，宏覆盖在多个基准上优于边际覆盖和类条件覆盖。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: long-tail distribution handling in medical image classification

quick_skim.title: HistoSeg++: Delving deeper with attention and multiscale feature fusion for biomarker segmentation
quick_skim.abstract: Segmentation of biomarkers in medical images is frequently viewed as a first step towards medical image analysis in any bioinformatics or biomedical application. Despite progress, existing methods still struggle to capture information at multiple scales and to perform upsampling effectively across different datasets. These shortcomings often result in suboptimal generalization capabilities. Recently, architectures belonging to the Nested-UNet family excel in capturing multiscale contextual information and upsample them effectively. In this work, We propose a novel Nested-UNet architecture that effectively captures multi-scale contextual information. It includes inner and outer attention units to enhance focus during upsampling, along with channel-wise feature recalibration using squeeze-and-excitation modules, leading to improved segmentation performance. Additionally, the architecture integrates an edge-aware loss to emphasize boundary accuracy by assigning greater importance to edge regions. Tested extensively on three publicly available benchmark datasets. Our method demonstrates a generalization performance superior to existing Nested-UNet methods. Code: https://github.com/saadwazir/histosegplusplus
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: deep learning with attention and multiscale feature fusion for medical image segmentation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用注意力机制和多尺度特征融合进行医学图像分割的深度学习方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用注意力机制和多尺度特征融合进行医学图像分割的深度学习方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用注意力机制和多尺度特征融合进行医学图像分割的深度学习方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HistoSeg++ proposes a novel Nested-UNet with inner/outer attention and multiscale feature fusion for biomarker segmentation in medical images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 医学图像生物标志物分割常作为分析第一步，但现有方法难以捕捉多尺度信息。本文提出改进的Nested-UNet结构，引入内外注意力单元增强上采样聚焦，有效捕获多尺度上下文信息。在多个数据集上展示了更好的泛化能力，为医学图像分析（包括眼底图像）提供了有力工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 医学图像生物标志物分割常作为分析第一步，但现有方法难以捕捉多尺度信息。本文提出改进的Nested-UNet结构，引入内外注意力单元增强上采样聚焦，有效捕获多尺度上下文信息。在多个数据集上展示了更好的泛化能力，为医学图像分析（包括眼底图像）提供了有力工具。

quick_skim.title: Inverse reinforcement learning reveals action-oriented value signals in naturalistic decision making
quick_skim.abstract: A major challenge for cognitive neuroscience is to explain how value of a goal-directed behavior is computed in complex and naturalistic environments. Standard computational models of decision making have been highly successful in controlled, trial-based paradigms, but they are often ill-suited to real-time behavior unfolding in naturalistic paradigms. Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) offers a way to infer latent evaluative state from observed behavior in naturalistic environments, but its neural interpretability remains largely unknown. Here, we investigated whether moment-to-moment reward trajectories derived from IRL map onto value signals in the brain during a real-time driving task performed during fMRI scanning. IRL-derived reward trajectories were most robustly associated with activity in the dorsal striatum, a region often linked to value-guided action selection. They also showed associations with distributed regions supporting additional processes, including cognitive control and sensorimotor processing. This pattern suggests that IRL reward captures distributed neural activity centered on the reward circuitry, potentially reflecting how valuation interacts with other processes. Together, these findings suggest that IRL reward provides a behaviorally grounded, temporally resolved proxy for action-oriented valuation during naturalistic decision making.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: inverse reinforcement learning for decision making
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用逆强化学习建模决策
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用逆强化学习建模决策
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用逆强化学习建模决策
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses inverse reinforcement learning to infer value signals from naturalistic driving behavior, linking IRL reward trajectories to brain activity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对自然环境中决策的神经机制问题，提出利用逆强化学习（IRL）从真实驾驶行为中推断潜在奖励轨迹，并验证其与fMRI脑信号的相关性。结果显示IRL衍生的时刻奖励信号与大脑价值区域活动匹配，为理解复杂决策的神经基础提供了新方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究针对自然环境中决策的神经机制问题，提出利用逆强化学习（IRL）从真实驾驶行为中推断潜在奖励轨迹，并验证其与fMRI脑信号的相关性。结果显示IRL衍生的时刻奖励信号与大脑价值区域活动匹配，为理解复杂决策的神经基础提供了新方法。

quick_skim.title: Zero-Shot Metabolite Prediction from Gene Expression via Physics-Informed Graph Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Predicting metabolite concentrations from gene expression is instrumental for linking regulatory programs to metabolic phenotypes. Prior approaches rely on static enzyme-metabolite mappings and often omit data-driven learning or biochemical constraints, limiting their ability to generalize to new metabolites. We present GAZE (Graph Attention for Zero-shot metabolite Estimation), a physics-informed graph neural network that integrates enzyme expression, Enzyme Commission functional embeddings, and ChemBERTa metabolite descriptors within a unified metabolic graph (5,414 nodes, 16,307 edges). A Metabolite-Conditioned Reader uses each metabolite's SMILES embedding to query learned pathway representations, enabling zero-shot prediction with no metabolite-specific parameters. We evaluate GAZE in three scenarios: (i) standard cross-validation on the Cancer Atlas of Metabolic Profiles (18,044 genes, 180 metabolites, 867 cell lines), achieving R2 = 0.816; (ii) leave-one-metabolite-out (LOMO) zero-shot evaluation across 50 held-out metabolites, where the physics-informed variant halves the median R2 deficit relative to a standard GNN baseline (-0.34 vs. -0.72), with 30% of unseen metabolites achieving positive R2; and (iii) external validation on an independent clear cell renal cell carcinoma tissue cohort (220 samples), where GAZE achieves median Spearman rho = 0.330 across 214 metabolites without fine-tuning. GAZE outperforms scCellFie, MEBOCOST, and UnitedMet across all evaluation settings.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: zero-shot metabolite prediction using GNN, similar to few-shot learning paradigm
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用图神经网络进行零样本代谢物预测，类似于少样本学习范式
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用图神经网络进行零样本代谢物预测，类似于少样本学习范式
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用图神经网络进行零样本代谢物预测，类似于少样本学习范式
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GAZE uses physics-informed GNN for zero-shot metabolite prediction, addressing generalization to unseen metabolites via graph attention and ChemBERTa embeddings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有代谢物预测方法依赖静态酶-代谢物映射，缺乏数据驱动学习和生化约束，难以泛化到新代谢物。本文提出GAZE，一个物理信息图神经网络，集成酶表达、EC功能嵌入和ChemBERTa代谢物描述符，通过代谢物条件读取器实现零样本预测。实验表明该方法能有效预测未见代谢物，为零样本学习在生物信息学中的应用提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有代谢物预测方法依赖静态酶-代谢物映射，缺乏数据驱动学习和生化约束，难以泛化到新代谢物。本文提出GAZE，一个物理信息图神经网络，集成酶表达、EC功能嵌入和ChemBERTa代谢物描述符，通过代谢物条件读取器实现零样本预测。实验表明该方法能有效预测未见代谢物，为零样本学习在生物信息学中的应用提供了新思路。
